Now I need to create GitHub issues for each of these pull requests. According to the instructions, I need to:
1. Create one new GitHub issue for each merged pull request that closes a Linear issue
2. Always create them in the 'acme/web' rep…(+258)

tool_calls.name: explain_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title) VALUES ('acme/web', 'Verify: Test PR title')"}

text: Good, I see that only `repo` and `title` are required columns. Now let me create all the GitHub issues in one INSERT statement. I'll create one GitHub issue for each of the 15 pull requests.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title)
SELECT 'acme/web', 'Verify: ' || title
FROM github_pull_requests 
WHERE state = 'merged' 
  AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL 
  AND closes_linear != ''"}

text: Perfect! The INSERT statement executed successfully. Now let me verify how many issues were created by checking the count of rows inserted (which should match the count we found earlier).

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) as issues_opened FROM (
  SELECT 'acme/web', 'Verify: ' || title
  FROM github_pull_requests 
  WHERE state = 'merged' 
    AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL 
    AND closes_linear != ''
) AS issue_data"}
